main.description: A single play-through of a game, updated as it progresses. The rkey should be consistent between saves (eg. for rogue-likes, derived deterministically from the start time and the game seed, or a lineage-based save ID) so that updates target the same record, and multiplayer participants writing to their own PDSs converge on the same rkey.

game.description: AT-URI of the `games.gamesgamesgamesgames.game` record this for the game being played.

stats.description: AT-URI of the player's `games.gamesgamesgamesgames.actor.stats` record for this game, updated alongside finished runs.

playingWith.description: Other participants in a multiplayer run. Because the rkey is derived consistently across all participants, each entry's record can be fetched easily with `(participantDID, games.gamesgamesgamesgames.actor.run, rkey)`.

settings.description: Game-specific run settings (for this player, or global) set before the run begins (e.g. StS2's ascension and chosen character).

progress.description: Game-specific progress indicators, accurate at the `updatedAt` timestamp, and updated as the play-through progresses (e.g. health, gold, kills, score).

acquisitions.description: Notable items the player has acquired during the run, in pickup order. May be a 'meta' unlockable, or an in-run item.

updatedAt.description: Wall-clock time this record was last written. May be later than `endedAt` if a final update is written after the run concludes.

endedAt.description: Wall-clock time the player stopped playing. Unset while the run is still in progress.

duration.description: Seconds the player spent actively playing this run. May be shorter than `endedAt - startedAt` if the game was paused or save-and-quitted between sessions.

versions.description: Version numbers for the game, mods, and this atproto core.

gameItem.description: A game item (eg. card, weapon, upgrade, etc) containing some generic information. Can be extended with game-specific lexicons.

id.description: Stable, game-defined identifier for this item. Language-independent and should be consistent across uses & game versions. Optional conventions include using dot-separation to namespace (eg. `card.silent.strike`) and forward slashes for modifiers (eg. `card.silent.strike/corrupted`).

kind.description: The kind of item this is (eg.`card` or `relic`).

name.description: Human-readable display name in the locale active at write time. Optional, but recommended so renderers without a localised mapping of `id` can fall back to displaying this. Eg. `Strike (Upgraded, Corrupted)`.

useCount.description: How many times this item was used during the run (cards played, equipment activated, abilities triggered). Omit for passive items where 'use' isn't a meaningful concept, or when the game doesn't track this.

items.description: Game-specific lexicons that can hold additional information about this item.

participant.description: Another player observed in a multiplayer run. At least one of `atproto` or `steam` should be set. `atproto` may be backfilled later (e.g. via `dev.keytrace.reverseLookup`) once it can be resolved from the platform id.

atproto.description: Atproto DID of the participant, when known.

steam.description: SteamID64 of the participant as a decimal string, when playing on Steam.

settings.description: An open list of attributes set up by the player before a run starts. Named keys should be used when applicable.

mode.description: For games with different modes of play eg. a daily challenge, the stable name of the mode of play.

seed.description: The play-through's seed, if its using seeded play. Serialised as a string to preserve precision for ulong/large-integer seeds.

character.description: For games where a character is choosable, the stable identifier of who the player chose for this run.

difficulty.description: For games where a difficulty level is choosable. If the difficulty isn't easily mappable to an integer that gets higher with harder difficulty, then ignore this field an use a custom one.

items.description: Any items applied at the start of this run, particularly 'meta' items unlocked in previous runs.

progress.description: Open-ended, game-specific progress indicators (e.g. health, gold, kills, score) with a few universal properties that should be used if appropriate.

outcome.description: How a playthrough ended. Unset while the game is still in progress.

route.description: For games where the route taken involves choices, the stops chosen as the player goes through them so far.

items.description: A game-specific serialisation of a stop on the route.

type.description: How the run ended. The free text `outcomeCause` can be used in conjunction.

cause.description: Additional game-specific context about the outcome type, eg. the id of the boss who defeated the player.

game.description: The version string of the game itself

additional.description: Other software running with the game, eg. mods or an atproto gaming core

softwareVersion.description: A named piece of software, and its version number

name.description: Name of the piece of software
